rows.title: VAR Dealer Demo Kit 7 Limitations
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith VAR Dealer Demo Kit 7 Limitations Product: VAR Dealer Demo Kit 7 KB: 1011762 Published: March 5, 2007 Last Updated: March 5, 2007 Summary The following table lists the limitations of the Dealer Demo products in the VAR Demo Kit. Laserfiche Product Limit per Session/Expiration Resetting Limits Laserfiche Agenda Manager 7.2 Published agendas are marked as being created by an evaluation version of Agenda Manager. Laserfiche Audit Trail 7.2.1 No Limits Laserfiche E-mail 7.2.1 100 pages Fixed limit per e-mail Laserfiche Bar Code 6.2 500 bar codes in one run Laserfiche Check Scanning (DCC) 6.2 No Limits Laserfiche Client 7.2.1 No Limits Laserfiche Administration Console 7.2.1 No Limits Laserfiche 6 to 7 Migration Wizard No Limits Laserfiche Document Classification 7.1.2 No Limits Laserfiche Firewall Compatibility Component 7.2 No Limits Laserfiche Forms Processing 7.1.2 No Limits Laserfiche Import Agent 7.0.2 500 files Restart the Laserfiche Import Agent service Laserfiche Image Annotation (Annotation/Bates Numbering) 7.1.2 No Limits Laserfiche Integration Express 6.0 Configuration: 1 Link Usage: 10 Searches Configuration: Delete the existing link Usage: Close and then reload Laserfiche Integration Express Laserfiche Integration Express - HTE 7.0 No Limits OmniPage® Zone OCR No Limits Laserfiche Pattern Matching 7.1.2 (Quick Fields Add-on) No Limits Laserfiche Quick Fields 7.1.2 500 pages Close and restart Laserfiche Quick Fields Laserfiche Real-Time Lookup 7.1.2 (Quick Fields Add-on) No Limits Laserfiche Scanning 7.2.1 500 pages Close and reopen the Scanning window Laserfiche ScanConnect 7.1 No Limits Laserfiche Server 7.2.1 LSAP Expiration Date Use beyond the expiration date requires LSAP renewal. Laserfiche Snapshot 7.0.3 25 documents Load the Laserfiche Snapshot Configuration dialog box Laserfiche Universal Capture 7.2.1 500 Pages Laserfiche Web Access 7.2.1 General limits: 3 simultaneous connections* No new connections can be established two hours after the first connection has been established. Display limits: 3 sticky notes per page Restart the World Wide Web Publishing service. Laserfiche WebLink 7.0.5 General Limits: 3 simultaneous connections* No new connections can be established two hours after the first connection has been established. All users can only connect from the same machine the first user connected from. Restart the World Wide Web Publishing service. Laserfiche Work Distribution Agent (Workflow Suite 7.0) 500 field events that trigger routing Restart the Laserfiche Server Automation service. Laserfiche Work Distribution Manager (Workflow Suite 7.0) 100 total destinations Fixed limit per rule. *Disconnecting a user through the Laserfiche Administration Console may not immediately free up a connection. Although the user connection may be disconnected from the Laserfiche Server, the connection between IIS and Web Access or WebLink must first time out. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Configuring Your Firewall for Laserfiche 8. 17 years ago a double colon, and then the custom port (e.g., 10.0.1.100:1888::2040). If a Laserfiche web product (i.e., Web Access and WebLink) cannot communicate with ... will recognize the Laserfiche Serv... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012084
rows.title: Configuring Your Firewall for Laserfiche 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Configuring Your Firewall for Laserfiche 8. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012084 Published: October 13, 2008 Last Updated: October 13, 2008 Summary Make sure that TCP ports 80 or 5050, 5051, and 1888 are open on your firewall. If your network contains multiple firewalls, please make sure that the listed ports are open on all firewalls between your Laserfiche Server and your Laserfiche client program. Note: You can use the Server Settings node of the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console to modify the default port settings for Laserfiche 8 and Laserfiche 6 client communication. More Information Laserfiche 8 client programs By default, Laserfiche Server 8 listens on TCP port 80 or 5050 for requests from version 8 client programs. Laserfiche Server 8 defaults to port 80 except when installed on the 32-bit edition of Windows XP, where Laserfiche defaults to port 5050. The Laserfiche Server broadcasts notifications on port 5051. Laserfiche 7 client programs The Laserfiche Firewall Compatibility Component is a utility that allows Laserfiche Server 8 to communicate across a firewall with Laserfiche 7 client programs. There are two components to this utility, one for the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server and another for the computer on which the desired Laserfiche client program resides. After installing the server component, you must set up how the Laserfiche Server will broadcast information. For each computer that cannot communicate with the Laserfiche Server, this configuration consists of installing the client component of this utility (if it is not already installed) and then setting up how each Laserfiche product communicates with the Laserfiche Server. Although the exact procedure that needs to be performed varies by product, the three basic ways to accomplish this are listed below At the Laserfiche Client 7 Start Page, attach each desired repository using the IP address for the Laserfiche Server, a double colon, and then the custom port (e.g., 10.0.1.100:1888::2040). If a Laserfiche web product (i.e., Web Access and WebLink) cannot communicate with the Laserfiche Server due to a firewall, then you will need to use the following notation when specifying how the Laserfiche product will recognize the Laserfiche Server: IPAddress:1888::Port (e.g., 10.1.1.100:1888::2040). The IP address and the FCC port (e.g., 2040) must be specified according to the values set in the "LfFccService72.exe.config" file on the Laserfiche Server. If Laserfiche Snapshot 7.0.2 is outside the firewall, then you will need to attach each desired repository using the Laserfiche client. Note: The client component of the Firewall Compatibility Component should be installed on the computer on which the Laserfiche product is installed. For example, if you would like to configure Laserfiche Web Access (version 7.0.1), then the client component only needs to be installed on the computer where Laserfiche Web Access was installed. Additionally, when configuring how Web Access will connect to a Laserfiche repository, you will need to specify the appropriate IP address, followed by a double colon, and then the custom port assigned to the Laserfiche Server. After installing the server component of the Firewall Compatibility Component, use a text editor to open the configuration file called "LfFccService72.exe.config." By default, this file can be found at: C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Server. Find the line that looks like: channel ref="tcp" port="2040" displayName="Laserfiche FCC Channel" Edit the port value and add the server machine's IP address to the line. The final result should look similar to the following: channel ref="tcp" port="2040" machineName="10.0.1.100" displayName="Laserfiche FCC Channel" The number "2040" should be replaced by the desired port that must be opened on the firewall. Keep in mind that this port cannot be used by another protocol. The IP address "10.0.1.100" should be replaced by the IP address that client machines will use to connect to the Laserfiche Server. Note: Be aware that if a NAT firewall is involved, this IP address may not be the actual IP address of the computer hosting the Laserfiche Server. Important: The Firewall Compatibility Component configuration file is case-sensitive. If the wrong case is used when modifying the configuration file, then the Laserfiche FCC 7.2 service will not function properly. Restart the Laserfiche Server and the Laserfiche Server Firewall Compatibility Component services. Configure the firewall so that the port specified in step 3 is open. Laserfiche 6 client programs Laserfiche 6 client programs use port 1888 for communication with the Laserfiche Server. If there is a firewall between your Laserfiche Server 8 instance and your version 6 Laserfiche client program, please make sure that port 1888 is open on your firewall. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

December 6, 2010 | KB: 1012716 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012716/a-quot-setup-re... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012716/a-setup-resource-decompression-
rows.title: A "Setup resource decompression failure" Error Occurs When Installing Audit Trail Reporting 8.
rows.detailTitle: A "Setup resource decompression failure" Error Occurs When Installing Audit Trail Reporting 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "Setup resource decompression failure" Error Occurs When Installing Audit Trail Reporting 8. Product: Audit Trail 8 KB: 1012716 Published: December 6, 2010 Last Updated: December 6, 2010 Summary When installing Audit Trail Reporting 8, you may receive the following error message: Setup resource decompression failure Cause This error can occur when the InstallAware installer framework is unable to extract temporary files to your Windows TEMP folder. Workaround 1 Verify that there is sufficient disk space available for your Windows TEMP folder and verify that you have sufficient rights to write to the Windows TEMP folder. If necessary, modify the Windows TEMP system variable to point to a location with sufficient disk space and where you have sufficient rights. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "6701: General Scanning Failure." Error Occurs When Scanning. 22 years ago A "6701: General Scanning Failure." Error Occurs When Scanning. July 6, 2004 | KB: 1000498 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000498/a-quot-6701-general-scanning-failure-quot-e... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000498/a-6701-general-scanning-failur ...
rows.title: A "6701: General Scanning Failure." Error Occurs When Scanning.
rows.detailTitle: A "6701: General Scanning Failure." Error Occurs When Scanning.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "6701: General Scanning Failure." Error Occurs When Scanning. Product: Laserfiche 6, Plus 6, Scanning 6 KB: 1000498 Published: July 6, 2004 Last Updated: July 6, 2004 Summary When you attempt to scan, you may receive the following error message: 6701: General Scanning Failure Cause The most common cause for this particular error is attempting to use a non-supported scanner with LaserFiche. Workaround There are three workarounds to this issue. Perform one of the following: Obtain a supported scanner. Contact Technical Support to see if your scanner is supported. If obtaining a new scanner is not a viable solution, you can still use your scanner outside of LaserFiche to first scan any documents. After scanning all documents, you can then manually import the files into the LaserFiche database. Start the LaserFiche Client and log into the appropriate database. From the File menu, click Import. Select the files you want to import and click Import. You can also use LaserFiche Universal Capture or LaserFiche Quick Fields to import the scanned files into the LaserFiche database. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "6531: Failure to log into database." Error Occurs When Configuring WebLink. 22 years ago A "6531: Failure to log into database." Error Occurs When Configuring WebLink. May 10, 2004 | KB: 1000556 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000556/a-quot-6531-failure-to-log-i... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000556/a-6531-failure-to-log-into ...
rows.title: A "6531: Failure to log into database." Error Occurs When Configuring WebLink.
rows.detailTitle: A "6531: Failure to log into database." Error Occurs When Configuring WebLink.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "6531: Failure to log into database." Error Occurs When Configuring WebLink. Product: WebLink 6 KB: 1000556 Published: May 10, 2004 Last Updated: May 10, 2004 Summary After installing WebLink, when you attempt to configure WebLink using the Web Products Administrator's Utility, you may receive the following error message: 6531: Failure to log into the database Cause WebLink is searching for a retrieval license through which it will log on to a LaserFiche database. However, the LaserFiche Server has only been licensed with full-featured licenses. Perform one of the following workarounds. Workaround 1 Users with WebLink 6.13 or later can enable WebLink to use both retrieval licenses and full-featured licenses. In the Web Products Administrator's Utility, under the General tab, select the Pool retrieval and full licenses check box. See the following article listed in the More Information section. Workaround 2 Purchase one or more retrieval licenses for the LaserFiche Server. More Information See the following related article: 1000524 Configuring WebLink to Use Both Retrieval and Full-Featured Licenses. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error 32002 - Unspecified failure has occurred. The form required to view this message cannot be displayed." Error Occurs When Sending E-mail. Product: E-mail 6 KB: 1000684 Published: August 23, 2004 Last Updated: August 23, 2004 Summary When you e-mail a document, you may receive the following error message: Error 32002 - Unspecified failure has occurred The form required to view this message cannot be displayed. Contact your admin Cause There is a known issue with Microsoft Exchange and Outlook involving a possible issue with the frmcache.dat file. Workaround For more information on this issue, please see the following Microsoft Knowledge Base article: 181930 Form Required to View This Message Cannot be Displayed Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "Access Denied." Error Occurs When Connecting To A Repository With Laserfiche Mobile 9. 12 years ago An "Access Denied." Error Occurs When Connecting To A Repository With Laserfiche Mobile 9. November 25, 2013 | KB: 1013368 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013368/an-quot-access-... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013368/an-access-denied-error-occ ...
rows.title: An "Access Denied." Error Occurs When Connecting To A Repository With Laserfiche Mobile 9.
rows.detailTitle: An "Access Denied." Error Occurs When Connecting To A Repository With Laserfiche Mobile 9.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Access Denied." Error Occurs When Connecting To A Repository With Laserfiche Mobile 9. Product: Laserfiche App 9 KB: 1013368 Published: November 25, 2013 Last Updated: November 25, 2013 Summary When connecting to a repository with Laserfiche Mobile 9, you may receive the following error message: Access Denied. Cause You may receive this error when the use does not have the Bypass Browse privilege or explicitly have the Browse entry access right on the root folder. The user, otherwise, has sufficient rights to entries within the root folder of the repository. Workaround Manually grant the Browse entry access right on the root folder for the affected user. Remember to use the This entry only scope when creating the access control entry. Open the Laserfiche Client and log in as an administrator with the Manage Entry Access privilege or the Write entry security entry access right on the root folder. Right-click on the root folder of the repository and select Access Rights. In the Entry Access dialog box, click Add. In the Create Access Control Entry dialog box, select the affected user account or a group that encompasses the affected user account. In the Scope Selection section, select This entry only. Click OK. Make sure the Allow check box is selected for Browse. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
